heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
The following numeric thresholds are binding for this project. All earlier draft values (N=24, N=15, N=10, α=0.05) are superseded and must be removed.

| Hypothesis | Null (H₀) | Alternative (H₁) | Test | Effect size | α | Power | N per arm |
|------------|-----------|------------------|------|-------------|---|-------|-----------|
| Old-centriole lineages proliferate less | μ_old = μ_young | μ_old < μ_young | Two-sample t-test (one-tailed) | d = 1.2 | 0.001 | 0.95 | 43 |
| Asymmetric divisions favour old centriole | p = 0.5 | p > 0.5 | Binomial test | HR = 2.0 | 0.001 | 0.95 | 43 |
| Centriole age correlates with senescence markers | ρ = 0 | ρ > 0 | Pearson correlation | ρ = 0.5 | 0.001 | 0.95 | 43 |

**Note:** These thresholds will be finalised after pilot data (N=5 lineages per arm) and registered on OSF prior to full data collection. Any deviation from these values will be documented as a pre-registration amendment.

## Pre-registration plan

**OSF ID:** osf.io/TBD
**Planned registration date:** 2026-07-01

**Pre-registration content (consolidated, single block):**
- Primary endpoint: proliferation rate (log2 fold change) in old-centriole vs young-centriole lineages
- Secondary endpoints: proportion of asymmetric divisions, senescence marker intensity
- Binding falsifiability thresholds: see unified table in §Falsifiability (N=43 per arm, α=0.001, power=0.95, effect size d=1.2)
- Statistical analysis plan: two-sample t-test, two-tailed, α=0.001, power=0.95
- Exclusion criteria: cell death, failed RITE labelling, technical artefacts
- Planned sample size: N=43 per arm (total N=86 lineages)
- Go/no-go decision criteria: based on unified falsifiability table only (no additional thresholds)

**Amendments:** Any changes to the above will be documented as a versioned amendment on OSF prior to data analysis.

**Linking note:** The pre-registration will contain the binding falsifiability thresholds and sample size calculation. The OSF record will be made public at registration.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
Sample size was calculated using the following formula for a two-sample t-test (one-tailed):

n = (Z_α + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²

Where:
- Z_α = 3.09 (for α = 0.001, one-tailed)
- Z_β = 1.645 (for β = 0.05, power = 0.95)
- σ² = pooled variance (placeholder, to be estimated from pilot data)
- δ = minimum detectable effect size (d = 1.2)

Using G*Power 3.1 (Faul et al., 2007), the required sample size is N=34 per arm. After adjusting for 20% attrition (cell death, failed labelling), the final sample size is N=43 per arm (total N=86 lineages).

**Note:** The variance estimate σ² is a placeholder. The final sample size will be recalculated after pilot data (N=5 per arm) and registered on OSF. If the pilot variance is larger than assumed, the sample size will be increased accordingly.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|-----|-------------|--------|------------|
| RITE construct fails to label centrioles | High (0.6) | Critical (project halt) | Test 3 independent RITE constructs in parallel; use alternative centriole markers (e.g., Centrin-GFP) as backup |
| Phototoxicity from repeated imaging | Medium (0.4) | High (cell death, biased data) | Use low-light microscopy (LED-based, <1% laser power); limit imaging to 2-hour intervals; include viability controls |
| FACS sorting by centriole colour fails | Medium (0.4) | High (no pure populations) | Validate FACS with known mixtures; use magnetic bead enrichment as alternative; include FACS purity check by microscopy |
| Asymmetric division rate too low for statistical power | Medium (0.3) | Medium (reduced power) | Increase observation window to 72h; use multiple fields of view; pre-register adaptive sample size increase |
| Centriole age marker bleaches during experiment | Low (0.2) | High (data loss) | Use photostable fluorescent proteins (e.g., mNeonGreen); add anti-fade reagents; include internal brightness controls |
| Contamination of cell culture | Low (0.1) | Medium (data loss) | Strict sterile technique; routine mycoplasma testing; maintain frozen backup stocks |
| Computational reconstruction of DAG fails | Medium (0.4) | Medium (no tree) | Develop and test reconstruction algorithm on simulated data first; use existing lineage-tracing tools (e.g., CellProfiler) as fallback |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
1. **RITE construct specificity:** The RITE system has not been validated for centriole labelling in mammalian cells. Off-target labelling or incomplete turnover could confound age measurements.
2. **Phototoxicity:** Even with low-light protocols, repeated imaging may stress cells and alter division dynamics, potentially biasing the observed lineage tree.
3. **FACS purity:** Sorting cells by centriole colour assumes a direct relationship between fluorescence and centriole age. Mixed-age centrioles within single cells could lead to misclassification.
4. **Generalisability:** Results from a single cell line (e.g., RPE-1) may not generalise to primary cells or tissues. Validation in at least one additional cell type is planned but not funded.
5. **Computational reconstruction:** The DAG reconstruction algorithm assumes perfect tracking of all divisions. Cell migration, death, or imaging artefacts will introduce missing data that may bias the tree.
6. **Centriole age as proxy for cell age:** Centriole age may not perfectly correlate with cellular age if centriole damage is repaired or if de novo biogenesis occurs. This assumption will be tested in pilot experiments.
7. **Statistical power:** The sample size calculation assumes a large effect size (d=1.2). If the true effect is smaller, the study will be underpowered. A pilot study will refine this estimate.
